text: Cambridge Report: Ethereum's Annual Power Use Falls to 7.87 GWh After The Merge According to a new report from the Cambridge Centre for Alternative Finance (CCAF), Ethereum consumes approximately 7.87 GWh of electricity annually following The Merge, a decline of more than 99.9% from its pre-Merge level. The report estimates annual emissions at around 2.37 ktCO₂e, down roughly 99.98%, based on an infrastructure audit of approximately 8,522 nodes. It also finds that the United States, Germany, Finland, and France host about 62% of Ethereum full nodes, while Hetzner, Amazon Web Services, and OVH together host roughly 40% of all nodes. In addition, 56.4% of the network's electricity is supplied by sustainable energy sources, with the report concluding that Ethereum's environmental footprint is now driven primarily by the carbon intensity of local power grids rather than its consensus mechanism. — link

text: South Korea's largest automaker completes pilot USDT stablecoin transfer on Avalanche Hyundai Motor, South Korea's largest automaker, became the country's first major company to use blockchain for real-time internal cross-border treasury transfers. The company completed a $20,000 USDT stablecoin pilot from its U.S. entity to its Mexico entity on Avalanche, cutting settlement time from several hours through traditional banking rails to about seven minutes. Hyundai plans to expand the mechanism to more countries and local currencies, and will launch a second pilot for its European subsidiaries later this month with Circle and Visa. — link

text: Prediction Markets Are Shifting Toward Entertainment-Driven Trading DWF Ventures said prediction market user behavior has changed significantly since 2024. The report said politics, elections and economics-related trading on Polymarket and Kalshi has fallen from about 73% and 96% of volume during the 2024 U.S. election to under 20% and 5%, respectively, while sports and parlay-style trading have grown. DWF Ventures warned that RFQ-based parlay products create information asymmetry and may push prediction markets away from their "truth engine" role, though higher recreational volume could also bring deeper liquidity and broader adoption. — link

text: QCP Market Colour, 10 July 2026 BTC has rebounded towards 64k after a sharp reversal in Japanese government bond yields eased concerns over a disorderly unwind of yen-funded carry trades. While the move provided relief for global liquidity, Japan's bond market remains a key risk with the BOJ meeting later this month. Crypto has also continued to absorb renewed geopolitical tensions. Despite the collapse of the US-Iran ceasefire, higher oil prices and a firmer dollar, BTC quickly recovered after a wave of leveraged liquidations pushed prices briefly towards 61.5k. The broader macro backdrop remains supportive but increasingly conditional. AI-driven growth, institutional demand and ETF inflows continue to underpin crypto, though next week's US CPI and the path of real yields remain key catalysts. BTC is showing resilience, but confirmation still requires a sustained move back above 64k. Is this the start of a stronger second-half recovery, or simply another pause before macro volatility returns? Read the full Market Colour here.

text: $CASHCAT sending and hit 180 mil. Still so many people offside on this trade assuming its "just another blockchain". Reread my last two posts, I try not to get overly bullish except this was such an obvious trade to take from a RR perspective. The narrative, chain and everything about it feels refreshing. Uniswap CEO is pushing this harder than anything he's touched on Eth and Arb in a long time, helped debacle the fud around the LP and tranches. Memes are where 95% of volume currently is. Id prefer a utility meta but I'm going to trade what the market gives me. Robinhood leaning into memes near the cycle bottom while predictions and sports betting grow stale. Attention is too valuable for future business moats. RH chain already flipped Hyperliquid in 24 hour Dex volume and growing substantially. Vlad has a lot of case studies to evaluate from failed predecessors. Considering how successful Hood has been, it's easier to assume he's done his homework on what sparks the fire and will keep it burning. Still think Cashcat has a good amount of juice in it based on how many people are offside and not conceptualizing the potential for the narrative. Only 14.5k holders so far and many catalysts left unturned. Even still I'm not a blind RH maxi and this is a momentum trade for me. A tip I'll give to those struggling to hit runners - take trades at face value. Discard what you've seen in crypto the past 12 months and cleanse your mind of previous port ATH's and stop being afraid to take sensible trades. If you're flip flopping on a narrative after initially being bullish, drop the size by 50-75%. Chances are your intuition was right but you've done mental gymnastics to psych yourself out of the trade. The earlier you can shake this the better as you'll capture more upside as the bull returns and risk switches on again. Removing the lag will have a massive compounding effect, not specifically on the number for your port, but the confidence of execution with precision.

text: Polymarket Seeks License to Offer Margin Trading Legally in US According to Bloomberg, Polymarket, the world's largest prediction market platform, is seeking US regulatory approval to offer margin trading, allowing users to open positions without posting the full amount of capital upfront. On July 3, Polymarket applied through its affiliate Coming Home GBA LLC for a futures commission merchant license with the National Futures Association. It must also obtain CFTC approval to amend its rulebook to permit non-fully collateralized trading. US rules would require users of prediction market margin products to undergo additional identity checks, including providing employer information. — link
